_scenario.name: Low-confidence / ambiguous message
_scenario.description: Guest sends an ambiguous greeting with no bookable intent. Bot should route to unknown or handoff_needed with low confidence, and respond by asking how it can help — NOT by guessing an intent and attempting a booking action.

_webhook.note: Use test-input path — Normalize Incoming Message node takes phone+guest_message directly when both present.

_meta_envelope_reference._note: Mode B (real inbound) would use this envelope instead of the flat test path above.

_assertions.expected_draft_behavior: Friendly greeting + offer to help: 'Hi! How can I help you today? Are you looking to book a stay, have questions about our packages, or something else?' Must NOT assume booking intent. Must NOT generate a payment link or hold.
_assertions.expected_handoff_reason: Low confidence — waiting for guest to clarify intent

_guardrail_check.note: Per §3x.8 — low route confidence → safe clarification question, NOT a silent no-op. Bot must not guess intent and attempt action. Confidence < 0.5 should trigger the low-confidence path.
_guardrail_check.pass_condition: Route is unknown or handoff_needed. Confidence < 0.5. Draft asks open-ended clarification. No booking action attempted.

expected_logging.workflow_events: ≥1 row: route=unknown or handoff_needed, low confidence, in payload

staff_review.knowledge_gap: Check: is the draft tone friendly and on-brand? Does it invite the guest to clarify without being robotic?
